thesis: AFTER-HOURS window (17:00-ET slot) — next-trading-day plan authored on the deterministic snapshot asOf 2026-07-23T20:48:20.711Z (~16:48 ET Thursday 7/23). The snapshot still flags marketClosed=false and reports snapshot.nextSessionDate = 2026-07-23, so per the deterministic roll this positions today's session into its close and overnight; today's 7/23 daily bar is NOT yet a confirmed settled close and is NOT counted for persistence. WHAT CHANGED vs the 15:00 ET pre-close run: the caution tell has EASED at the margin — VIX pulled BACK to 18.7 (band 18.7) from 19.48, back OUT of the 19-20 zone the prior runs flagged as the calm-regime falsifier and now nearer the pre-market 17.67; the calm regime is RE-FIRMING rather than being tested harder. But the tech-led damage has NOT repaired: SPY still sits BELOW its 50-day and QQQ/VGT/MSFT stay below theirs, and the stretched semis leader SOXX FADED back from the pre-close 'Basing' to WEAKENING/out. STRUCTURE FIRST: the extended tech-led structural bull remains intact on completed bars; today's rejection persists but is holding, leadership stays DEFENSIVE/ENERGY. Prices (LIVE INTRADAY / into-close, today's 7/23 bar not settled): SPY 738.18 is -0.79% BELOW its 50-day (~744.1) — a touch firmer than the pre-close -0.9% but still under it — while bull-stacked on the longer MAs (+6.2% over its 200-day ~695.1, mom126/252 +8.27%/+18.68%) and -2.57% off the ATH (~757.7). QQQ 691.96 holds its breakdown at -3.67% below its 50-day (~718.4, TANGLED, mom5 -1.98). VGT 114.39 tangled (-1.8% below its 50-day) with its 5-day now marginally positive (mom5 +0.13) — the tech selling keeps decelerating, not reversing. MSFT 381.58 sits -4.5% below its 50-day (~399.6), bear-stacked, mom63 -11.67. THE RELATIVE HOLD PERSISTS: SPMO 149.83 keeps its bull MA-stack (20>50>200, mom5 +3.13, +17.19% over its 200-day) though fractionally under its 50-day (-0.45%) — broad momentum held while tech beta was sold, confirming rotation over liquidation. SEMIS ROLLED BACK: SOXX rrgState Weakening ↓ / rotation out (rsMom 99.82<100, rsMom1m -9.27, above-50 3/12, up-momentum 3/12) — the pre-close basing attempt did NOT hold; still +39.21% over its 200-day but no reclaimed leadership. Persistence by distinct COMPLETED daily bars: ~10 (7/09->7/22) — 7 directly confirmed (7/09->7/10->7/13->7/14->7/15->7/16->7/17) plus 7/20/7/21/7/22 settled via the recentHistory date-roll chain; today 7/23 has NOT closed (marketClosed=false) — its bar is forming, this rejection is LIVE tape, NOT a completed bar and NOT counted; intraday reruns are not counted. ROTATION (owning tool REFRESHED this run, sector-research-lab asOf 2026-07-23T20:48:20Z): reads 'Rotate toward XLE as XLV weakens' (11 sectors scored). Leadership is DEFENSIVE/ENERGY: XLE Leading and BOARD-BEST (rsMom 102.92, rsRatio 101.14, accel +2.32, into, +15.17% over its 200-day) alongside rate-sensitive/defensive leaders XLU (rsMom 101.81, accel +1.89, into), XLRE (rsMom 101.51, accel +2.24, into), XLI (rsMom 101.01, accel +1.57, into), XLF Leading neutral (rsMom 100.25, accel -0.06), plus XLP (rsMom 101.1, accel +1.94) and XLB (rsMom 101.09, accel +1.73) Improving into — versus XLK LAGGING (rsMom 99.57<100, accel +0.04), XLV Weakening (accel -1.23, out), XLC/XLY Lagging. MAGS Lagging (1/7 bull-stacked, ma200Dist -2.38). GLOBAL/REAL-ASSETS (owning tools carried from earlier today 13:30Z, same session): global-rotation-lab Asia/global-led on a local-currency (FX-headwind) basis — EWS Singapore 77.5 leads vs ACWI, EWC Canada next; real-assets-lab shows NO precious-metal haven bid (GLD 14, SLV 0, BTC-USD 41.7 mixed) but a clear ENERGY/COMMODITY lead (PDBC 70.6 leads, DBC 70.4) confirming the XLE rotation; etf-momentum-lab has SPMO leading the 6M balanced ranking (27.91%, 13 scored). TACTICAL, last: monthly OPEX (7/17) is RESOLVED and dealer gamma has RESET — prior pin/wall/magnet values are DEAD; fresh post-OPEX gamma walls, the overnight/into-close pin/magnet, option-implied moves, VIX9D/VIX3M term structure and the live 7/29 FedWatch path (carried ~53.5% hold / ~46.5% hike) are NOT in this run and MUST be re-pulled before sizing any gamma trade, vol-term claim, or Fed positioning. After-hours single-name reaction prints are not surfaced separately in Tier-A this run. PLAN for the next trading day and overnight: HOLD the longer-term structure but do NOT add index beta while SPY is below its 50-day (require a confirmed daily CLOSE that reclaims it, not the into-close tick); TRIM stretched tech/semis beta into any bounce (QQQ/VGT below their 50-days; SOXX rolled back to Weakening); ROTATE toward the confirmed defensive/energy Leading 'into' cluster (XLE board-best and commodity-confirmed, plus XLU/XLRE/XLI/XLP/XLB) funded from weakening XLV/XLK; HOLD (don't chase) MSFT below its 50-day into the 7/29 capex guide; carry the small defined-risk hedge overnight toward 7/29 — with VIX easing to 18.7 it is slightly LESS pressing than at pre-close but still warranted into a live two-way Fed, keep it small/cheap — after re-verifying the live path; stay OUT of precious-metal havens (GLD 14 / SLV 0). Tomorrow's catalysts: no high-importance macro release scheduled for 7/24 in Tier-A; the nearest cluster is 7/29 FOMC + MSFT AMC.
